title: SSC CHSL 2026
meta_description: SSC CHSL 2026 — 12th pass government jobs as LDC, Postal Assistant, DEO. Exam pattern, salary ₹20-35K/month, typing test, and preparation.

hero.one_liner: Government jobs for 12th pass candidates — LDC, Postal Assistant, Data Entry Operator in central government departments with ₹20-35K salary

what_is_it: SSC CHSL (Staff Selection Commission — Combined Higher Secondary Level) is an annual central government recruitment exam for 12th pass (10+2) candidates. Application window opens April 30, 2026, and Tier 1 exam is scheduled for July-September 2026. It recruits ~6,000 candidates for Group C posts: Lower Division Clerk (LDC), Postal Assistant (PA), Sorting Assistant (SA), Data Entry Operator (DEO), and DEO Grade A — all in central government offices, post offices, and CAG departments.

CHSL is one of the most accessible government exams because it requires only 12th pass qualification — no graduation needed. The two-tier structure is simpler than competitive exams: Tier 1 (online MCQ screening) and Tier 2 (online merit exam + typing/data entry skill test). Unlike SSC CGL, CHSL doesn't have an interview, making selection purely merit-based on written performance + skill test.

The Tier 1 pattern is IDENTICAL to SSC CGL Tier 1 (100 questions, 200 marks, 60 minutes). This means you can prepare for both exams simultaneously with 90% common syllabus. Many candidates use CHSL as a backup while preparing for CGL or banking exams. Passing CHSL is statistically easier than CGL due to fewer skilled competitors.

Starting salary for LDC (Level 2): ₹22,000-28,000/month. Postal Assistant (Level 4): ₹25,000-32,000/month. DEO Grade A (Level 5): ₹29,000-35,000/month. While lower than banking exams, CHSL posts offer genuine career growth: LDCs get promoted to UDC (Under Division Clerk) after departmental exams, then Assistant, eventually reaching supervisory positions.

key_details.label: Education
key_details.value: 12th pass (10+2) from recognized board. Final year students eligible.

key_details.label: Selection
key_details.value: Tier 1 (CBT, qualifying) → Tier 2 (CBT, merit + Typing/Data Entry skill test)

exam_pattern.description: Same as SSC CGL Tier 1. 100 MCQs, 200 marks total. Purely qualifying — marks don't count in final merit ranking. Candidate's relative performance determines Tier 2 eligibility.
Tier 2 determines your final ranking and post allocation. Two sessions with different papers for different posts. Typing/Data Entry test is qualifying (pass/fail, not scored).

major_posts.post: Lower Division Clerk (LDC) / Junior Secretariat Assistant (JSA)

extra_sections.heading: CHSL vs CGL vs Banking PO — Which Exam Should You Take?
content: CHSL is your primary exam. You can appear for SSC CGL only after completing graduation.
CHSL offers genuine career progression: LDC → UDC (promotion exam) → Assistant (further promotion) → supervisory posts. Total compensation at highest level: ₹70,000+/month.
You can appear for both SSC CHSL and SSC CGL in the same cycle. CGL offers higher posts (Level 4-8, salary ₹25,000-60,000+).
Strategy: Use CHSL as insurance while targeting CGL. Tier 1 for both is IDENTICAL (100 Qs, same difficulty), so no extra effort.
Passing CHSL is statistically easier (lower cutoffs) than CGL; guaranteed success rate is higher.

Banking PO: Starting salary ₹40-68K in-hand, but competition extreme (~20 lakh applicants, 0.1% selection rate). CHSL: Starting salary ₹22-35K in-hand, competition moderate (~5-8 lakh applicants, ~0.1% also, but easier to crack).
Banking offers faster salary growth; CHSL offers guaranteed career in government. Choose: Higher risk-higher reward (Banking) vs Safer, stable (CHSL).

extra_sections.heading: Typing & Data Entry Skill Test — How to Prepare
rows: English typing 35 wpm or Hindi typing 30 wpm
Practice 30 min daily for 3 months with free typing websites
Practice data entry from format sheets, 45 min daily for 3 months

preparation_tips: Since Tier 1 is identical to CGL Tier 1, use CGL preparation resources: NCERT 6-10 for GK, RS Aggarwal for Reasoning, Kiran's Math for Quant, Wren & Martin for English. Online: Adda247, Testbook, Physics Wallah.

Practice typing daily from Month 3 onwards. For LDC: You need 35 wpm (words per minute). Start with accuracy (aim for 99% correct), then build speed. Use free websites: TypingMaster, Keybr, or TypeRacer (gamified learning). 30 minutes daily for 12 weeks = solid pass rate.

For DEO post: Practice data entry (8,000 key depressions/hour = 133 characters/minute). This requires speed + accuracy. Use software like MS Excel to practice entering data quickly. Or find data entry practice websites. 45 minutes daily practice sufficient.
Solve previous year Tier 1 papers (2018-2024) — SSC repeats patterns heavily. Solve at least 15-20 papers before exam. Speed matters: 1 question in 36 seconds average. Time yourself while solving.

faqs.question: Can graduates apply for CHSL?
faqs.answer: Yes. CHSL eligibility is '12th pass OR equivalent OR higher' — so graduates can apply. Many graduates use CHSL as backup for CGL. However, post allocation is based on qualifications: Graduates get higher-level post options; 12th-pass candidates get standard posts.

faqs.question: Is there an interview in CHSL?
faqs.answer: No interview. Selection is purely based on Tier 1 + Tier 2 + Skill Test scores. This makes CHSL MORE objective than exams with interviews. Your written performance alone determines selection.

faqs.question: What if I fail the typing test after clearing Tier 2?
faqs.answer: You are not selected. Typing test is qualifying (pass/fail, not scored), but you MUST pass it to be appointed. If you fail, you can reappear in the next CHSL cycle. This is why typing practice is critical — don't neglect it thinking 'it's just qualifying'.

faqs.question: Postal Assistant — is it a good post compared to LDC?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's better. PA is at Pay Level 4 (₹25,000-32,000 starting), while LDC is Level 2 (₹22,000-28,000). PA work: counter operations, stamps, savings schemes, money orders. Posting usually in home state/nearby. Promotions to Inspector Posts (Level 7+) are possible. PA is considered a more skilled post than clerk work.

faqs.question: How many attempts are allowed for CHSL?
faqs.answer: Unlimited, as long as you meet the age limit (27 years for General). You can keep appearing every year. Each attempt is experience — don't hesitate to reappear.

faqs.question: What's the expected cutoff for Tier 1 in CHSL 2026?
faqs.answer: Based on recent cycles: General category typically 95-105 out of 200 (47-52%). OBC: 85-95, SC/ST: 75-85. These are estimates; actual cutoffs depend on candidate pool and difficulty. Aim to score 110+ to be safe.

image_suggestions: Career growth chart from LDC to higher posts

syllabus.subject: General Intelligence & Reasoning
syllabus.topics: Analogy, classification, series (number/alphabet/figure), coding-decoding, direction sense, blood relations, order & ranking, matrix, Venn diagram, missing numbers, embedded figures, mirror/water image

syllabus.subject: Quantitative Aptitude
syllabus.topics: Number system, percentage, ratio, profit & loss, average, SI/CI, time & work, time & distance, algebra (basic), geometry (lines, angles, triangles, circles), mensuration (2D + 3D), trigonometry (basic identities), data interpretation

syllabus.subject: English Language
syllabus.topics: Reading comprehension, fill in the blanks, error spotting, sentence correction, synonym/antonym, idioms & phrases, one word substitution, spelling correction, active-passive voice, direct-indirect speech

syllabus.subject: General Awareness
syllabus.topics: Indian history, geography, polity, economics, current affairs, general science, static GK (awards, books, sports, important days), computer basics

study_plan.tasks: Complete basic maths from R.S. Aggarwal (first 15 chapters). Start vocabulary from Norman Lewis (20 words/day). Read Lucent GK daily 1 hour. Reasoning basics — analogy, series, coding.
Solve previous year papers (2018-2025). English grammar practice — error spotting and fill in blanks. Current affairs compilation. Speed practice — target 3 minutes per question.
2 mock tests per week. Analyze mistakes. Revise GK notes and vocabulary. Target: 150+ out of 200 in Tier 1 for safe selection. Last 2 weeks: revision only.

quiz.question: Select the odd one out: Cat, Dog, Rat, Rose
If APPLE is coded as ELPPA, then MANGO is coded as:
A clock shows 3:15. What is the angle between the hour and minute hands?
Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
